faq.question: How much does solar cost?
faq.answer: With $0 down financing, there's no upfront cost. You pay a fixed monthly amount that is typically lower than your current electricity bill — and you lock in your rate for 25 years.

faq.question: How long does installation take?
faq.answer: Most residential installations are completed in 1–3 days once permits are approved. We handle the entire permitting process for you, which typically takes 2–4 weeks.

faq.question: What if my roof isn't ideal for solar?
faq.answer: We perform a thorough site assessment before recommending anything. If your roof isn't a great fit, we'll let you know honestly — we never push a system that won't deliver real savings.

faq.question: Do I qualify for tax credits or rebates?
faq.answer: There is currently a 30% federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC) available on solar installations. Additionally, San Diego Community Power offers battery-specific rebates — funds are limited, so we recommend applying soon. We walk you through every incentive you qualify for.

faq.question: What happens during a power outage?
faq.answer: If you have a battery backup system (like Tesla Powerwall or Enphase), your home stays powered during outages using stored solar energy. We can design your system to prioritize your most essential appliances.

rebate_alert.heading: Limited funds available for battery rebates & credits
rebate_alert.text: Apply as soon as possible before they expire to see if you qualify. San Diego Community Power currently offers upfront rebates of $250–$500 per kWh on new battery installations, plus ongoing performance incentives — but funds are limited and availability is not guaranteed.

blog_posts.title: What the Trump Administration's Solar Policy Changes Mean for San Diego Homeowners
blog_posts.body:
Federal solar policy is shifting — but the opportunity for San Diego homeowners to go solar may actually be bigger than ever right now. Here's what's changing, what's staying, and what you should do about it.

## The Federal Landscape Is Changing

The Trump administration has signaled a clear intent to roll back certain clean energy policies, including potential changes to the Investment Tax Credit (ITC) and other federal incentives that have made residential solar more affordable over the past decade. While the details are still evolving, the direction of travel is clear: federal support for solar is likely to shrink.

But "likely to shrink" doesn't mean "gone tomorrow." And for San Diego homeowners, there are several reasons why acting now — rather than waiting — could mean the difference between thousands of dollars saved and thousands left on the table.

## What's Still on the Table Right Now

As of today, the **30% federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC)** remains in effect for residential solar installations. This means if you install a solar system in 2026, you can claim 30% of the total installation cost as a credit on your federal tax return. On a $25,000 system, that's $7,500 back in your pocket.

There's no guarantee this rate will hold through the end of the year at current political dynamics. Locking in your installation sooner rather than later is the smartest way to secure that credit.

## San Diego Has Local Incentives Too

Beyond the federal ITC, San Diego Community Power currently offers a **Solar Battery Savings program** that provides upfront rebates on qualifying battery installations:

- **$350/kWh** for new solar + battery systems (market rate customers)
- **$250/kWh** for adding a battery to an existing solar setup
- **$500/kWh** for non-market rate customers (CARE/FERA enrolled)
- Plus **$0.10/kWh performance incentive** when your battery supports the grid during peak hours

These funds are limited and are not guaranteed to last. If you're a San Diego Community Power customer, this is one of the best battery rebate programs available anywhere in the state right now.

## What This Means for You

If you've been on the fence about solar, the current political climate actually makes a stronger case for acting sooner. Here's the logic:

- The **federal ITC is at 30%** — the highest it's been in years, and potentially the highest it will be for a while.
- Local battery rebates are **funded and active**, but subject to budget constraints.
- Solar panel and battery prices have **come down significantly**, making this one of the most cost-effective times to install.
- California's electricity rates continue to climb, meaning **the savings from going solar grow every year** you wait.

## The Bottom Line

Federal policy uncertainty is real. But it's also exactly why now is the time to move — not later. The incentives that exist today may not exist in six months. The savings you lock in by installing now compound for 25+ years.

If you're a San Diego homeowner and you've been thinking about solar, we'd encourage you to at least get a free quote. There's no obligation, and knowing your numbers is always a good first step.

faq.question: Do I need sales experience?
faq.answer: No. We provide full training for people new to sales. What matters most is your willingness to learn, work hard, and grow.

faq.question: How much can I really earn?
faq.answer: It's commission-based, so your income depends on your effort. Our top performers consistently earn $6K-$10K per 10-day cycle. New reps typically ramp up over their first few weeks.

faq.question: What's the schedule like?
faq.answer: You set your own schedule. Most reps work evenings and weekends when homeowners are available, but the hours are flexible.

faq.question: Is this door-to-door or online leads?
faq.answer: This is primarily door-to-door canvassing in San Diego neighborhoods. We match you with territory in areas you know.

faq.question: What kind of support do I get?
faq.answer: You'll have ongoing coaching, weekly team check-ins, and direct access to leadership. We're invested in your success.
